# Preserving your work Save a copy of files you create during this task to `/logs/artifacts/`. This directory is preserved after the run so outputs can be inspected. Organize with subdirectories if helpful (e.g., `/logs/artifacts/code/`, `/logs/artifacts/reports/`). The directory already exists and is writable. --- # tRPC v11 Next.js App Router Data Fetching ## Background In tRPC v11, `createCallerFactory` replaces the old `createCaller` to execute procedures on the server (e.g., in React Server Components). You need to set up a Next.js App Router project that fetches initial data on the server and provides client-side interactivity using React Query. ## Requirements - A Next.js App Router project is located at `/home/user/app`. - The tRPC router is defined in `src/server/trpc.ts` with a `hello` query that takes a `name` string and returns `Hello ${name}`. - Use `createCallerFactory` to create a server-side caller in `src/server/caller.ts`. - In the Server Component `src/app/page.tsx`, use the caller to fetch `hello({ name: 'Server' })` and render it inside an `

` tag with id `server-data`. - Provide a client component `src/app/ClientComponent.tsx` that uses `trpc.hello.useQuery({ name: 'Client' })` and renders it inside a `

` tag with id `client-data`. ## Implementation Guide 1. Modify `src/server/caller.ts` to export a `caller` created using `createCallerFactory`. 2. Update `src/app/page.tsx` to be an async Server Component, fetch data using the caller, and render it. Also, include ``. 3. Update `src/app/ClientComponent.tsx` to use the tRPC React Query hook. ## Constraints - Project path: `/home/user/app` - Start command: `npm run build && npm start` - Port: 3000
